stories.title: U.S. says it seized an Iranian cargo ship, ceasefire expires soon
stories.description: The two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire Wednesday. And the two sides seem far from a resolution. President Trump says the US has taken custody of an Iranian vessel.

stories.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.

stories.title: Born in south Lebanon, displaced to Beirut, two grandmothers reflect on Israeli invasions
stories.description: They grew up amid olive groves in southern Lebanon. The son of one married the other's daughter. Now they're living temporarily in a vacant building in central Beirut, displaced many times.

stories.title: US intercepts and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship, Trump says
stories.description: Tehran has not commented on the incident, which comes as the US prepares for a second round of talks.

stories.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
stories.description: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

stories.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.description: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.description: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.title: UK and France to lead defensive mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Sir Keir Starmer has said further details of the plan will come after a meeting in London next week.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: The Israeli town on the frontline with Hezbollah
stories.description: BBC foreign correspondent Nick Beake visits Metula, an Israeli town surrounded on three sides by Lebanon.

stories.title: Middle East war live: Iran vows response after US destroyer fires on cargo ship
stories.description: Iran vowed retaliation after a US destroyer fired on an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman on Sunday, as it was attempting to evade a US naval blockade. Tehran condemned the incident as “armed piracy” and warned its forces would respond soon. Follow our liveblog for t...

stories.title: Trump says US delegation will travel to Islamabad on Monday, Iran declines talks
stories.description: Iran is not currently planning to attend the next round of talks with the US, which has announced that it would send a team of negotiators to Pakistan on Monday, Iranian state television reported on Sunday.

stories.title: Trump sending US delegation for Iran talks in Pakistan
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said US negotiators will head to Pakistan on Monday for another round of talks with Iran, raising hopes of extending a fragile ceasefire set to expire by Wednesday even as Washington and Tehran remain in a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz. There ⁠is ​c...

stories.title: 'Ceasefire in the Middle East, especially in Lebanon, are meant to be broken', analyst says
stories.description: In the midst of a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, new rounds of talks are due to take place in Washington to determine whether the truce can be extended. However, for Aaron David Miller, Senior Fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, ceasefires in t...

stories.title: Iran asserts leverage in Hormuz ahead of new round of talks
stories.description: Iran has partially reopened the Strait of Hormuz, but only under strict authorisation and designated routes, as tensions with the US continue. While Pakistan tries to organise new talks, Iranian officials insist sanctions must be lifted and reject US demands to end nuclear activi...

stories.title: Israel's double game between negotiations with Lebanon and occupation in the south
stories.description: A ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon was reached following a first round of talks in Washington. Hussein Ibish, Senior Resident Scholar at the Arab Gulf States Institute in Washington, analyses Israel’s strategy in Lebanon, which he describes as oscillating between ne...

stories.title: Food security under threat as Middle East war disrupts fertiliser supply
stories.description: Around one-third of global fertiliser production comes from the Gulf region, making the area’s stability crucial for global food supply chains. With the Strait of Hormuz, an essential transit route for energy and commodities, facing repeated disruptions, concerns are growing over...

stories.title: A petition called 'Lebanon will not be the next Gaza' calls on French leaders to take action
stories.description: As France's President Emmanuel Macron is seen as a key guarantor of the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, civil society actors have launched a petition titled 'Lebanon will not be the next Gaza', calling on him to act. The initiative has been signed by numerous French public ...

stories.title: Iran war live: Tehran slams US ‘piracy’ after ship seizure, vows response
stories.description: Attack comes hours after US President Donald Trump announced he is sending his team to Islamabad for possible talks.

stories.title: Outrage after photo shows Israeli soldier smashing Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: Social media users condemn Western silence on attacks on religious symbols and sites by Israeli soldiers and settlers.

stories.title: US forces attack and seize Iranian ship Touska near Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: US Central Command has published a video said to show a guided-missile destroyer firing at an Iranian-flagged ship.

stories.title: Argentina’s Milei backs US-Israel war on Iran in Jerusalem visit
stories.description: Argentina’s president reaffirmed alignment with the US and Israel during a visit to Jerusalem, backing their war on Iran

stories.title: Schools, shops shut in northern Israel to protest the Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: Shops and schools shut in northern Israel as residents protested a 10-day ceasefire with Lebanon.

stories.title: Protesters defy Berlin rain to call for end to global wars
stories.description: Hundreds protested in Berlin against the US-Israeli aggression on Iran, and Israeli attacks on Gaza and Lebanon.

stories.title: Trump says US seized Iranian ship trying to get past blockade near Hormuz
stories.description: Iran vows retaliation, accuses US of violating ceasefire after attack on cargo ship Touska.

stories.title: Did the US misjudge Iran? A military strategist answers
stories.description: Strategist Bryan Clark examines shifting war aims, escalation traps, and whether Iran is now more dangerous to the US.

stories.title: Trump says US representatives heading to Islamabad, will be ‘there tomorrow evening for negotiations’ with Iran
stories.description: President Donald Trump announced on Sunday that representatives from Washington were heading to Islamabad and they would be “there tomorrow evening for negotiations” with Iran. The US president made the announcement in a Truth Social post, where he also insisted that Washington w...

stories.title: Indian officials push for regional energy hub in Sri Lanka
stories.description: Indian officials called for the swift implementation of a proposed regional energy hub in Sri Lanka at the start of bilateral talks in Colombo on Sunday. The planned oil hub in the northeastern port of Trincomalee featured in a meeting with Sri Lankan President Anura Kumara Dissa...

stories.title: Israeli military publishes map of new deployment line in Lebanon, bringing dozens of villages under its control
stories.description: The Israeli military published for the first time a map of its new deployment line inside Lebanon on Sunday, bringing dozens of mostly abandoned Lebanese villages under its control, ​days after a ceasefire with Lebanon took effect. There was no immediate comment from Lebanese off...

stories.title: Security beefed up as Islamabad prepares for foreign delegations’ arrival amid anticipation of US-Iran talks
stories.description: Islamabad’s Red Zone was closed for traffic as the capital braces to welcome foreign delegations, police said on Sunday, with alternate traffic plans issued to facilitate citizens. “Due to the arrival of foreign delegations in Islamabad, Red Zone and Extended Red Zone will remain...

stories.title: 17 immigrants have died in US custody this year: ICE
stories.description: WASHINGTON: At least 17 immigrants have died in US Immigration and Customs Enforcement custody from January 2026 through early April, according to ICE. That follows 31 deaths last year, a two-decade high. Aled Damien Carbonell-Betan­court of Cuba died on April 12 at a federal det...

stories.title: France blames Hezbollah for soldier’s death in Lebanon
stories.description: • Hezbollah denies allegation, calls for ‘caution in making judgements’• Macron urges Beirut to ‘guarantee security’ of UNIFIL peacekeepers PARIS / BEIRUT: A French soldier was killed and three others wounded in an attack on Saturday on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon that appeared to...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war live: Iran accuses U.S. of ceasefire breach, vows to 'soon respond' after U.S. destroyer hits cargo ship
stories.description: Iran is not currently planning to attend talks with the United States on Monday

stories.title: Iran-Israel war updates: No plan for Tehran to attend new talks with U.S. in Pakistan, says Iranian media
stories.description: Donald Trump said that a U.S. destroyer ship fired on and seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman that tried to evade a U.S. naval blockade

stories.title: Iran not planning to attend talks with U.S. in Pakistan
stories.description: Donald Trump announced that a U.S. destroyer fired at a massive Iranian-flagged cargo ship after it tried to get past the U.S. naval blockade

stories.title: India-flagged vessels start sailing out of Persian Gulf
stories.description: Most of these ships are critical to India’s energy needs and are carrying crude oil

stories.title: Trump says U.S. negotiators will be in Pakistan on April 20 for talks with Iran
stories.description: U.S. Vice-President J.D. Vance will lead the U.S. delegation for talks with Iran in Pakistan, a White House official said — shortly after President Trump said he would not make the trip

stories.title: Zelenskyy slams oil sanctions relief for Russia
stories.description: That is $10 billion -- a resource that is directly converted into new strikes against Ukraine, says Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy

stories.title: Iran’s military deliver drone strikes on US warships in response to its vessel’s seizure
stories.description: According to the news agency, Iran's military intended to continue responding to "piracy" policy and attacks on behalf of the US side

stories.title: US not serious about conflict settlement by posing threats to Iranian vessels — Araghchi
stories.description: The Iranian top diplomat added he believed that "the contradictory stance and violations of the ceasefire regime on behalf of the United States speaks for America's ill intentions"

stories.title: Iranian president, Pakistani PM discuss situation in Middle East
stories.description: Masoud Pezeshkian stressed that Pakistan, with the support from its partners, remains committed to "its role as an honest and sincere facilitator of lasting peace and regional stability"

stories.title: Iran refuses from engaging in second round of talks with US — agency
stories.description: The Tasnim agency said that Tehran will make a decision on whether to send its negotiating team to Pakistan only after Washington lifts its naval blockade or Iran
